ingredients: 8 oz sashimi-grade fish (e.g., tuna, salmon, yellowtail), well-chilled
steps: Keep fish refrigerated until just before slicing; pat dry and remove any pin bones.
Using a very sharp knife, slice fish against the grain into even pieces (about 1/4 inch thick), using long, single strokes.
Arrange slices on a chilled plate with optional daikon garnish.
Serve immediately with soy sauce, wasabi, and pickled ginger.
tips: Only use sashimi-grade fish from a trusted supplier; keep everything cold and use a razor-sharp knife for clean cuts.
